reviews.review_msg: Professor Scott MacEachern is the best prof in SFU. The course ran both synchronously and asynchronously. I did not do good on the first midterm, yet I still enjoyed learning accounting. He does not give tricky questions- the ones covered in class and homework are testable! If possible, take a course with him. You will not regret it.

Scott is the GOAT. Funniest guy you will ever meet. His videos are seriously entertaining. He turns a boring subject like accounting into something fun. Highly recommend taking a class with Scott if possible. Hes a really nice guy and our whole class loved him to death

Fall20. Best prof so far during remote. Makes the best video lectures. Pre-recorded videos are multiple short videos, so its easy to maintain focus. His tone captures your attention unlike other boring profs. I thought Ill get bored in this class cos its Accounting, but no, he has excellent teaching style, very modern. Exams are well written.

Scott is hands down the best prof I've ever had. Lots of weekly homework and readings for a 3 unit course but overall the homework-type questions are fairly similar to the midterms and final. Would 100% recommend taking any course with Scott!

Great professor. Made this class easy to understand. Midterm was very easy however the final was tough. So far my favourite prof at SFU. He keeps lectures interesting and is always willing to help. Gives students chances to redeem themselves for hw and quizzes.

You can tell Angela tries her best to deliver good lectures but teaching remotely is not a friendly method. Attending her office hours is helpful, email? bruh. Besides, she requires specific answers to essay questions, so the average is always low. Worth taking compared to the other profs who teaches BUS251.

She wants very specific answers, so even if your answers are correct, you're not getting points if you use different wording. Does not provide exam or quiz answers and reviews after they're done. Does not clearly answer emails. Lectures are extremely boring but that is subjective. Unproductive learning experience.
She is a horrible instructor, doesn't help if you have any doubts. Going for her office hours is completely useless.
Angela is super nice!!!! Just choose her.

As opposed to her Bus 251 counterpart, Angela is a way better choice. Her lectures, although boring, are somewhat useful in a sense that you can use them to get ready for both exams. The finals worth 50%, so definitely pay more attention to the post-midterm material. Just dont skip any classes and DO ALL OF THE PRACTICE PROBLEMS and youll be ok.
Angela is a nice Accounting instructor! Do practicing !!!
Good prof and TERRIBLE course.
Easy course.
Attend the class. Listen to the lecture. Ask questions if you have. Angela is a reliable instructor as long as you want to do well in accounting. She lets you make effort for your grade.

Angela is a very experienced accountant. Relatively a much better choice for BUS251 (You know what I mean). She is nice, glad to answer questions and listen to students. Do not miss the class because the participation counts 10%. Make sure do the notes as well as finish the homework and you will be fine.

I never went to class js read the textbook and did the nailed down the wiley questions, they were really good help for the midterm and final. midterm was light, the final was a bit more tough. Overall wiley helped more than the canvas quizzes. There isn't tutorial attendance either.

good prof for a hard class he gave us a cheat sheet for final, it was nice he adjusted our grades if we improved on final and performed better than midterms. He's not trying to fail you so work hard. use Wiley! many questions were from his weekly homework, do practice questions per chapter and format of exams is same as the practice ones he gives
i could not understand him for the whole year sadly. nice guy tho. course is pretty difficult anyways
While he is chill, he does not teach very well and you are left to fend for yourself on all the assignments. Without the TA's this class would be in shambles
hes funny and a good enough prof. gave pizza party to distract u from the fact that his final is worth 50%.... n sometimes he lwk sounds like he speaking chinese but other than that hes chill
honestly he's kinda cute

If you want everything spelled out to you.. she is not very clear. But I felt like I learned all the concepts very thoroughly with a lot of independent work. Tutorials were EXTREMELY helpful. Homework was EXTREMELY helpful. Don't fall behind in this class! The midterm and final were really not that hard if you had not fallen behind.
Midterm was hard, but she made the final a bit easier. Awesome prof. Extremely helpful and really knows what she is talking about. Definately recommend!
Amazing professor. Funny too. Extremely helpful. I liked the course so the exams were easy. Breezed through the class.
One of the best prof I ever had....Listen to the lecture take notes, read first 3 chapters and u good to go)
One of my best prof!!!Come, Listen. take notes, ask a question, and thats it))But make sure u read first 3 chapters....

Nice professor. Stay on top of readings and do the homework, tutorial and in-class questions to prepare for exams. Tried to make lectures entertaining and interactive, and he provides lots of practice materials.
bro wants us to memorize a million and one formulas and know the exact formatting and layout of like 6 different statements
Really kind and knowledgeble professor, he made me no more afraid about this course
Took for 321. Great prof, exams were very fair, not too challenging but also not too easy. No projects or papers which is nice, just very short weekly assignments and two exams.
Mr Mowbray seems to genuinely care for his students's wellbeing and encourages them to take on useful challenges which was evident in his courses. Recommended!

Grant was a great professor and made accounting much more easy than I thought. I took it in Summer of 2024 and it was a flipped class where you do all your readings and assignments outside of the class online and then come into class and ask questions. 

His exams were very fair and were very similar to his practice exams. Do those a ton!
Good luck with him

Would recommend taking 251 with Grant, he went over practice problems in class and provided good resources to help with midterm and final. He was also always willing to help after class or via email. Tests were fair and similar to the practice exams. Course content was heavy so make sure you put in time to study each week to do good.

Great guy, great prof. He took an inherently dry subject in accounting and was able to add some personality and humor to it. Not saying the lectures were fun, but they weren't super boring. He does a great job at explaining concepts, and takes lots of time for questions. He also provides lots of resources (practice tests, recorded lecs, etc)

Come to class and do your readings.  The professor is fair but the volume required by this course is enormous.  He tells you at the beginning if you don't have enough time to dedicate to this class, don't take it. This is completely accurate.  If you don't have 6-7 hours a week to dedicate to this class, drop it.
Just listen to the lecture and always practice the questions (no surprised questions in the exams), and you will get a very good grade. He is a nice guy and very cares about his students!
Funny guy. Felt like his rating was too low.
This rating is for BUS  252.

Grant does come across somewhat inexperienced as a lecturer and his explanations of topics is sometimes lacking. That said, he is approachable and willing to help. His tests are not easy so expect to put in a lot of preparation.
Amazing professor, was taking accounting for the first time in my life but he made it look soo easy

Ilia's class is so much fun as long as you follow up and do the work, I can certainly say it was one of my favourite classes. I was so happy each day to wake up and go to his class. He created a respectful and calm environment for everyone. He inspired me a lot in both life and educational aspect. Definitely never seen a professor as caring as him!
I had both 251 and 312 with him, he was pretty good in both but I think better in 312..
He is Funny....
Got A+ nice Guy take it with him
Highly recommended
He was very helpful and always available for extra help!made the class enjoyable and understandable
Attends every lecture and relies on youtube videos to understand the material. Funny how 5 minutes videos explain so much better than her 2 hour lecture.

Very self-directed teaching method. Lectures will cover a topic like an income statement, tutorials will review textbook questions regarding income statements, but when it comes down to creating and putting appropriate information into an income statement, you must learn that yourself through the textbook. Previous ACCBUS experience  is recomended
her name is actually Anne MacDonald and she is highly recommended for 251.
gpa killer
not bad
I wish that I can take another Accounting class with him!
Great great professor. Giving students proper practice of problems.
Go to class,finish your homework and there is an A+
Nice prof very easy grader but final is too long
he is a very helpful prof...not only teacher, but also my friend~

Richard was a great professor and very engaging, helped students to get a good understanding of financial accounting, just keep in mind that the examinations required good knowledge of the questions rather than just memorization of formulas, as there might be some harder concepts, but overall good prof.

My favourite class and professor to date, his assignments truly prepared you for the class and his exams were exactly the content of the lectures (harder questions of course but nothing insane). If I can tell you one thing as the key to success in this course, its study lecture slides, attend and participate in tutorial, and try hard the Wiley+!!!!
Great prof, no more comments to be made honestly.

GOAT prof fr 🐐. Hard course but as someone who never gets anything, I actually understood shiz here 🤯. For that, Richard gets 5 BIG BOOMS — BOOM BOOM BOOM BOOM BOOM 💥.  Not as funny as I expected, but he's still the real one, the legend, the professor we needed. 

TAs? Straight-up chaos — sassy little gremlins. Straight-up mood swings on turbo.

Richard was by far one of the best professors I've ever had at this point for the entirety of my university career. He's very kind and caring and clearly puts a LOT of effort into ensuring his students succeed. His lectures are always very well done, and I never really had any issues understanding the material he taught. He made the cours enjoyable

Richard is an amazing prof, for such a difficult course. He breaks down super complex topics and provides many practice questions for understanding. He is also accessible by email, and is very kind. Would 100% recommend Richard for BUS 251!!!

Richard was probably one of the best professors I've had at SFU. He genuinely wants you to succeed and was very clear at giving lectures. Great introduction to accounting for me. He also is engaging with the lectures and isn't boring at all. 10/10 (or i guess 5/5)

i prefer him over praise ma. richard breaks down difficult concepts extremely clearly. puts emphasis on practice, practice, practice and gives marks for completing practice assignments. you will not get confused so long as you go over his ENTIRE slidedecks every week and DO HIS PRACTICE Q'S. 

an excellent teacher given the difficult subject.

I'm a genuine person. Like all of us, I procrastinate, am lazy, etc. But this class is good because the lectures are actually quality. It's guaranteed that if you go to the lecture, you won't have to read the textbook ONCE

just read the slides and focus in class. For assignments he gives the answers in his slides as most accounting is template
Not great.
Its impossible to do bad in his class if you just pay attention. Made accounting incredibly easy. In class examples are great. Use those to study and you'll get above average everytime.
BEST professor for BUS 251 EVER!!!!!

Richard is a great professor and he cares about your success. He is happy to chat/help in office hours. He doesn't waste your time and expects the same in return. Accounting can be intimidating but his lectures and slides are great and I suggest not bothering with the textbook. I suggest relying heavily on his lecture slides and example questions.
Micheal Jordan

Richard is a great professor! He tried his best to make lectures engaging, including accounting memes, and really drove in important concepts. Nothing was unexpected on the exams, and other marks came from tutorial participation, weekly homework, and periodic knowledge checks. This is literally the only course that I've attended every lecture for.
Very good professor, loves to crack jokes in class. Makes class interesting. However he goes very quick in class so you must stay up to date with all class materials.
I really hate accounting but at no fault of Richard. Exams are fair, explanations are straightforward, and class is fast-paced without being boring. Highly recommend.
Never read the textbook, just go over lecture slides and understand all in class questions and you'll be fine. lectures are entertaining.

Really good professor. Honestly no complaints about Richard. He speaks clearly, he knows what he's talking about, he's accessible consistently, and never doesn't have an answer to a question. Just make sure you actually work on the online homework assignments and don't just rush them. It will help you for exams

Great instructor. Accounting is tough - the math is really easy but the way of thinking is hard to learn.
Class is curved so I am not sure I can say it's hard or easy.

I can imagine the class to be way more boring without Richard
Very fair. Class is on a curve but lots of people find accounting hard so if you are strong quantitatively, this will probably be an easy A for you. Definitely requires lots of time and practice.

Great professor for a tedious class. Well spoken and very punctual. Fair marker. My advice- don't waste the Wiley+ homework assignments- they are valuable and help study for the exam. Take your time with them and don't rush to look at your notes or the lecture slides. It's a good trade off to struggle in the homework but do well in the exams.

Best professor to take BUS 251 with, lectures are super engaging, its so hard to skip a lecture, lectures are content heavy but they are taught extremely well as they are spaced out. The exams are very very fair, you just need to practice from day one and you will nail this course.

Although I didn't enjoy his weekly assignments, they forced me to study the content weekly which helped me succeed on exams. The exams reflected assignments and especially his "Additional Selected Practice Problems". He is enthusiastic in his lectures and has us try problems in class. He explains concepts clearly and was helpful in office hours.
Thank you Richard!!! My first A was gave by you!

Richard was an amazing professor! I took BUS251 and I really liked it; in his tutorials your participation will be counted as attendance so make sure to participate in every tutorial! Sometimes his homework's can be challenging, however, I can assure you that if you assist to his office hours or his TA's office hours you should be fine!
Excellent! I never met a more funny, organized, and responsible instructor like him.
The best professor ever for any accounting course at SFU. If you choose others, you will regret it that much!!! Strongly recommond for all business students!
Amazing and funny prof. Richard cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Highly recommend!

Should probably be a 4 credit course given the workload. Needed to practice a lot to get good at it - so can understand why the homework was weekly. 

Richard was delightful. Worth it to have him as an instructor alone.

He is a good prof and the lectures are understandable, but the workload is pretty heave and it makes it so hard to manage. weekly assignments and knowledge test and other assignments. the class is gonna take so much of your time don't take this class if your taking a full coarse load

Honestly, accounting is not my cup of tea but accounting was my FAVOURITE course last semester. Richard is engaging and packs a lot of stuff in a 2-hour time span. I loved his approach of providing us with in-class questions to practice. If anyone can convince you to go into accounting, it's Richard.

Richard is one of the best profs I have had he makes the class more enjoyable and the dad jokes kept me engaged. he does a good job splitting harder materials into smaller sections which helps understand the materials. the midterm and final were fair too and relevant to the practice done in class which helped for the exams
Such a great guy. Explains concepts clearly, plenty of examples, and engages with the class. He's got a whole arsenal of wicked dad jokes, difficult not to laugh out loud sometimes. He's my god.
Her lectures were boring. Luckily, they were not mandatory or necessary at all.  Her exams were not the same format as the homework, and were carefully graded, make sure you understand the material

Praise is a gem. Her lectures were good, super clear, asthetic slides. Her exams were tough (Averages in low 60s), but fair, the questions were always very similar to the assigned tutorial questions, so if you do your work you'll be above curve. She is approachable and kind, and genueinely goes the extra mile for her students. Highly recommend.

Praise is an okay professor. She isn't horrible. I found her slides BAD, and it's hard to look over. She does care as a professor, but she makes the class more confusing than it needs to be. She tends to rush over important topics, & her exams are fair (mostly) but frustrating because you need to STUDY EVERYTHING (no good hints on what's on it).

Praise is a really kind person and she GENUINELY cares about her students and their learning experience. She incorporates Kahoots and practice problems in lectures to make it engaging, as well as recording and posting all lectures on Canvas. As long as you do the practice problems and homework assignments every week, you'll be okay!

Personally i dislike how she structures her slides and introduces topics. too much going on, she tries to use so many unnecessary graphics and jam them into everything that it's difficult to comprehend what the actual main concepts and themes are. the content itself is easy, but the prof really overcomplicates it

Truly a great professor, my personal favourite, she is so nice and cares for all students! She made accounting very easy to understand and provided manyyyy resources to learn from, WileyPlus made learning much easier. My favourite class, did very well thanks to Praise!
Great Prof. Top 3 Prof I have taken at sfu in my 4 semesters. Don't hesi to take. Everything is recorded, can't think of a single thing to ask to imrpove on it was honestly perfect.
Outstanding professor who goes out of her way to help students. I highly recommend her if you want fair exams and engaging lectures - just beware that they can be quite fast-paced sometimes.

she's a great prof, provides all the material you need to succeed and is extremely thorough. the only determinant is whether you put in effort or not. however, i dislike her lecture style; it's quite fast-paced and i find her slidedecks confusing to decipher. the amount of information can be overwhelming but just study hard and youll be fine

Praise is a great professor, her lectures are informative and engaging. Her tests can be a little tough, ensure you understand all the concepts and frequently complete practice questions. Beware of the final being 40% of the grade. Overall, very kind and caring prof.

Praise was a very fun and engaging professor overall. She cares about your success. However, she goes through the lecture material way too fast, making it really hard to keep up. Also, tests are 70% of your final grade. I wish she would put more weight on the assignments and explain concepts better because this class was challenging for me.

One of the best accounting professors out there in SFU. I took her last fall semester and the concept explained by her throughout the lecture was obvious and understandable. She is also easy to reach through email and during office hours and helps students a lot to get good grades

Praise Ma is a phenomenal professor, she is very energetic and makes lectures very engaging for a dull subject like accounting. She's very caring if you show up to her office hours she will go the extra mile to help you. Assignments on wileyplus are fair but tough at times, her in-class examples are useful. Overall a very positive experience
A living legend. If you can't handle the heat, get out of the kitchen. Steve is an example of Accounting upper division courses at Beedie.

Does not teach anything about accounting and already assumes you know what to do. Rambles a lot in class about Taylor Swift, Elon Musk, and how America is so great and China being inferior. You will learn more about BUS251 in youtube, just look for "Tony Bell" channel and you will have everything you need.

Just RUN
He is not a good speaker and have no passion in teaching, always degrading students tell you you are not smart enough to pass the course.
Did that one review said he is easy after failing 2 midterms lol
No no no guys. Just run...no no...
Never met this instructor made the midterm exam like this hard.....
GOOD LUCK!!!! Horrible.....
Run for your life! ! ! DON'T TAKE IT! ! Just leave him without any reason. Otherwise, you will fail from our respectful "Steve-o & Gibco" company owner :)
It was perfect, down to the nearest minute detail. Very good prof, amazing notes and challenging homework and samples. This course is not for the weak tho.

The prof is not for softies and encouragement-seeking weak-minded individuals. Yes, the course is super hard and yes, the prof makes it harder. However, I still loved every second of the 13 4-hours lectures because of the based jokes from this guy. Your grade and your ability to get along the prof will determine if BBA is really for you.
Best male accounting prof ever, kind nice, knowledgeable, hardworking

Very helpful professor, he is young and nice. even spend a hour for one student's question. assignment is well related with exam, midterm is not hard, but the final is disaster. Really nice grader, gives more A than others.

Very helpful and nice professor who cares about his students. Tries his best to engage the class during lectures. Course is structured well. Doing the weekly homework questions and going to lectures are crucial to doing well. Midterm and final were fair.

Very dedicated to helping students understand the material, which can be quite dry. Midterm was fair but the final was quite a bit more challenging. Lecture slides are clear and helpful. If you have to take this course, take it with him.

Despite the fact that I don't enjoy accounting, he tried very hard to make it interesting for everyone. His passion for his job is impeccable; if you were to find a model prof with good attitude and not smug, he would be the first choice. Yes he has a Chinese accent but I guess it's just a give and take. If I had a choice, I would take it again :)
highly recommend!!!!

You can tell that Wing is highly interested in the subject and wants his students to succeed! He provides simple examples to apply to sometimes complicated concepts which makes it so much easier to understand. His exams are mostly based on the lecture notes. He was able to make the bland content of this course interesting :)
GREAT PROF (despite the accent)he try his best to make everything simple and easy, just make sure you do the assignments and actually UNDERSTAND them, then your good to go..
Awesome prof!The guy who made me interested in accounting again!Very dedicated prof as well,patient and always try his best to make study material understandable to everyone.Highly recommended!
No Comments

Teaches you information as if you can't read the book or notes. Hector, his TA, teaches this class WAYYY better than he does because he actually has NOTES. Lectures complete waste of time and so is the prof. Used to like accounting until now..worst prof ever and SFU as well as yourself are better off with ANYONE else
His powerpoint notes are word for word off the textbook. His class is as stimulating as a clipped nail and it's really true that you must teach yourself.

WORST teacher EVER...His class is so pointless i stopped going after the midterm. He clearly has no idea about what he is teaching, and just reads the powerpoint slides. It is a hard class, and you must be able to teach yourself if you want to pass. Dont take him as a professor!!!!!!!!!

Not only boring and pointless,but annoyoing.All the reviews have touched on the essentials but one more wouldnt hurt,only help.DONT TAKE this class with CRAIG EMBY-he reads out the powerpoints which he gets from the txt book cd,no clarity,just repetition,no insight-Not a prof,rather an unhappy Acct.
boring lecture, hard exam, never go to his class after the 1st lecture.i think we should fire him.waste of my time and my money.
I've had worse... but, I agree with what everyone else has said... the lectures were not necessary... this class is basically an online class.  Good luck on the final everyone.

WORST PROF IN THE WORLD!! DOESN'T POST A SAMPLE EXAM BECAUSE HE'S TOO LAZY.. TAKES OFF THE LAST WEEK OF CLASSES..DOESN'T SHOW UP TO ANY OF HIS OFFICE HOURS DURING EXAM WEEK! LECTURES ARE BASED ON THE POWERPOINT SLIDES AND HE DOESN'T ELABORATE AND HE CAN'T EVEN FIGURE OUT WHAT THEY'RE SAYING AT TIMES

i stopped going to his classes after the 3rd week.  no point in going and wasting 2 hours of your life each week.  he doesn't teach anything.  everything's from the powerpoint you get in the textbook.  i self taught myself for this course.
Prof doesn't go beyond the text book, it's really difficult to stay awake in his lecture. Though his midterm was fair, as long as you read the text you're good to go. Lectures are quite pointless.

I think this class is super boring.  You basically teach yourself since lectures are straight from the text.  Class is unecessary as long as you do the readings and the assigments, you'll do fine on the exam.
HORRIBLE PROF!! lectures are worthless.. goes through powerpoint slides that came with the book without elaborating.. u basically have to teach urself difficult concepts that are hard to decipher
worst/most boring prof ever all you need to do is to bring your pillow, find a comfortable position, and sit for the whole class
the most boring lecture I've ever had in my university life don't need to go to lectures at all because all you need to do is to sleep in class

No need to go to the lecture, all he does is repeat the exact same thing from the textbook. But be sure to read EVERYTHING from the textbook, even the stuff he doesn't cover, because he will put up some 25-mark questions like that on the final any time.
The most boring 2 hours a week of my life.  Very dull lectures, straight from the book.  You can miss 90% of the classes and be no worse off.

Great prof, explains everything well and gives ton of practice questions that help with the exams. She provides lectures slides and goes over them in lecture and are quite straight forward.  Also, sets up additional review sessions before final. And will weigh the final more if you do better on it than you did on the midterm!

Maureen's very kind and will answer your questions thoroughly if you go to her office hour. Midterm was relevant to textbook questions but final was much more difficult. Her 8-hour review sessions weren't helpful as the exam was nothing like the questions she covered. I would recommend her class but you definitely have to work hard.

Her slides are very vague so if you skip class, you'll really struggle with the way she teaches and how she will test you on it. She doesn't really follow the textbook and her exams are hard, but as long as you study, you should be fine. She replies emails fast & if you go to office hours, she will be there to help. She's a sweet and caring prof.

Slides contain basic concepts but some parts are too unclear and simplified, esp for midterm and final. Like learning abc or 123 and expecting complete literacy. Repeating points in the end won't help for hyper rush lectures. I understand Prof and TA want us to learn, but they are condescending and adds little encouragement. Not recommended at all!

I felt like her teaching wasn't bad and she explained the topics pretty well but she was not accessible at all, don't reply to any email and same thing with the TAs. I had a question about the final and nobody answered me at all
Dont even bother just wait for Praise Ma's course. Test questions had maybe a 1-6 chance of being part of the homework that was assigned.
Don't bother taking this class wait for a semester when she isn't teaching it. Nothing she shows for practice or tutorial is resemblant of what is on the test
Curved the final so well she's a gem.

Wow. Just WOW. She is very impatient and doesn't show respect to others. She'll get mad at you for asking questions in class, claims she is never wrong, and threatens the class with "I don't have to be here you know" if she gets unhappy. No one finishes the exams on time and the exam average is just horrendous. Avoid at all costs!!!
Her exams are so difficult. If you get chance to take with another Proff , do it!!!
I enjoyed my time in her class. She would tell us what was important to study for the midterms and final exam. Her notes were sufficient, but if you want an A reading the textbook is required.
I read the reviews before taking this class and thought no way it can be that bad. I was wrong, DO NOT take this class with her.
I enjoyed taking 251 with her. I also found the coursework fairly easy.

I've seen happier people in prison and her included. Looks like she's doing this for free for how much she cares about questions and her lecture style. Never seen a more unenthusiastic or careless prof or teacher in my entire life. Looks like she just witnessed murder before this with that blank stare. Do yourself a favour and get out.

Don't ever take this class with her u wont be doing yourself a favor. She can't explain lectures well, and her grading is strict. On the final exam, she had to correct so many typos, just wasting our time.

Lectures were frequently cut short, tutorials were not well organized, and exam questions were often more difficult than the assigned homework. Material covered on the final was unclear. I found this course difficult, not because of the content itself, but how it was delivered.

This was my first accounting course at Beedie. She simply couldn't teach it. She didn't even know what she was talking about. It was simply incomprehensible. Please resign and don't impact the faculty's reputation.

Take this class with any other professor. Her exams her impossible. Attendance for tutorials are worth 25% of your grade but they were pointless as you were split into groups every class to complete problems that no one knew how to do. The TA's did nothing in tutorials to help you learn. Susan is also very rude when students ask a question.
This class was extremely hard with Susan. Do not take it with her. I had never seen some of the questions she asked us to do on the final. rough class

Absolutely brutal. Every class felt like getting scolded for existing, she explains nothing, answers questions with a disgusting tone, sass, and condescending tude. Asking a question gets you a glare and a lecture about “figure it out yourself", and on the final she stopped taking questions midway bcuz she was tired of walking up the damn stairs😭

Susan couldn't teach a fish how to swim. She gets angry when you have a question and expects you to know everything. TA's are brutal and don't speak English. If you have to take this class with her, don't go to lecture, complete waste of time

I only selected the hilarious button because this course with Susan is laughable. You have to laugh, or else you will be crying like some people were when they handed in their final exam. Seriously, the worst exam I have ever taken at SFU. Hard class overall, but even harder with Susan. I wish anyone luck who enrolls in this class.
Don't even think about it. Don't take this class with her. Save your mental wellbeing. Please

Do not take this course with her. The final exam was impossible, and nothing like we had seen in class. The TAs are not helpful, and doing the homework doesn't really help you in the end. The tutorial exercises also don't help. Taking the class with Susan is not a good idea, select any other prof if you can.

Susan is hands down the worst professor I have ever had in my life. She is rude, condescending and passive-aggressive. She makes you feel you're stupid if you dont know how to do something. Her exams are NOTHING like the homework. Avoid this class with her at all costs. Her final exam is brutal, people walked out crying.
Note to prof: if you give us 8 million survey questions we're not going to fill them out accurately....u're wasting ure time. Random marking, useless lectures
makes the class more boring than it already is. tough exams.
She's good. But you better do much much more than what's required of minimum. Otherwise, you are screwed.

Accounting is hard but she gives us problem sets for exam practice to help us prepare. I would recommend doing the WileyPlus consistently and asking TAs questions in office hours if you have any because they will go more in depth. In lecture, Anne goes more in depth and gives real life examples. Spring 2023 was her last term at SFU.

Professor MacDonald was an amazing lecturer and was very engaging. There was lots of homework but it was good practice for the exams. She gave detailed outlines for homework and projects. Exams were super fair and she gave practice exams that were almost identical in format to the actual exams. This professor made accounting interesting.
Just bad, like every other SFU professor, don't take.

The prof was alright a little better than average. One thing that she did that I found odd was that she would post incomplete slides on canvas. This meant that you had to go through the lecture recordings which was a waste of my time. Very fair prof but didn't do anything extra to make this course memorable.
She's a really good teacher and does a good job. Her lectures can be a bit dry, but also long as you keep up with the textbook, it's pretty easy to follow along

Anne is easily one of the best profs--explains main concepts clearly, adding fascinating insight on real-world applications. So patient & kind in office hours and exams are VERY fair. Know theory&technical aspects + understand WHY info is useful to shareholders. Use wiley online questions and go to office hours--TAs & prof are so nice and helpful!!

Has many resources to help you prepare for midterm and final. Weekly quizzes and online assignments, occasional longer assignments, one group presentation, midterm, and final. Takes a lot of time outside of lecture just because of the amount of assignments and length of textbook readings.

absolutely dreaded having to take 251, but WOW Anne completely changed my mindset. An amazing prof who is experienced, fair, and straight to the point. Her lectures are the only ones I find engaging throughout, and she does a great job in making accounting interesting. I'm surprised to say that 251 is now my favorite course this semester.
Nice guy
I was taking this class for the second time, Susie made me love boring accounting

Demanding class.  Weekly assignments take up a lot of time, but ensure that you keep up with the readings.  I went to class only 3 times and still got an A.  Use all the online resources, the text, her notes, and study hard and you will do well.  She doesn't play tricks on her exams, she wants you to do well.
Very hard teacher who assigns a lot of work. Must do readings if you want to pass. She speaks too quickly in lecture and is very dry.
He is hard marker, but really helpful

worst prof ever, extremely rude, and doesn't like being challenged. if you want to pass this course, do not take it with this prof. he will not only ruin your mood, but he will make you want to kill yourself, and him in the process. do not make the mistake of taking this course with this prof. you have been warned.

His accent is terrible to understand! You do not know what to expect in the finales and midterms! I noticed that if you want to get a good grade in this subject, you must already know this subject! Personally, i want to cry after his lectures!
I found that you do need to go to lectures and tutorials. Do the practice questions on a weekly basis to not fall behind. Overall, thought the course was fair.
His lecture is very in details. Although he has a little accent, it's understandable. The most important thing is that he always has patience.

the midterm not so hard however he was very picky marking with theoretical questions, which pulled many students marks down. the final was much harder than the midterm. he makes things more difficult than they actually are. Overall, not a bad prof but not a good prof either.

Lazy prof (fails to provide practice for final exam), confusing in lecture.  I could basically learn the exact same thing with the Wiley program.  All he does is read from Wiley and confuse you more with craptacular examples he ingeniously makes up... sorry masood you let me down

Nice prof, very clear with his notes and lectures. Altho he teaches according to ppt, it's still a good idea to attend his lectures cos sometimes he throws in things like "this is important, study 4exam!" exams are really fair, so if you read the text & do pract problems, you should be fine :) try to get him as your TA too! he's awesome.

Steve is truly one of the amazing professors at Beedie School of Business. He made the course very fun and easy to understand. He truly cares for his students and goes above and beyond to see them succeed. The course itself is not that difficult either. Just a couple of hours per week are enough to get a good grade. All the best for your journey!

My journey with Steve was perfectly perfect. He genuinely cares for his students. He mixes humour with the course contents which makes even the boring lectures super fun. In order to catch up with the course, you must read the chapter after its done in class. Honestly, I would love to take another class with Steve, he's just a great professor.

Professor Steve is the best professor so far. He makes the lectures so fun and easy. People who gave him a low ranking probably don't study well after his class. Trust me 2 hours of studying per WEEK is enough to get an A in this course. He's more like a friend than a professor. He also gives bonus quizzes to boost tour grade. :)
he is the best professor as he fuels you with the negativity that he gives you i highly recommand to people who can't do things.

He is the worst professor ever and I will never ever take a course with him even if I don't have any choice. He is awful and thinks his students know everything. He will never give feedbacks and he is like a torture to students. DO NOT take this course with him!
He is the worst professor Ive studied with. Would never take him again. Hes always gonna give a negative feedback and tries to look down on students all the time.

This man is the worst. Doesn't even encourage participation because he is so intimidating. He thinks he looks so cool but in reality he is just dumb. Tell him hi, he will hardly respond to you. Secondly, this prof forgets some of the stuff he teaches and tries to refer back to his NOTES!!! 3rd, when he explains he isnt even look at the class. :@
